title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Dry Throat
intro: Herbal teas for dry throat are a type of medicinal tea made from various herbs, flowers, and plants that help soothe and relieve dryness, irritation, and discomfort in the throat. These teas can effectively treat dry throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and moisturizing properties, which help calm the throat and reduce inflammation. Some examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat dry throat include slippery elm, which forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, licorice root, which has anti-inflammatory properties, and peppermint, which helps to thin mucus and provide relief. Additionally, other herbal teas such as ginger, chamomile, and thyme can also be used to treat dry throat due to their soothing and calming effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used to treat dry throat ailments due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and relieve dryness in the throat, providing quick and effective relief. The bioactive constituents, particularly glycyrrhizin, possess expectorant properties that help to thin mucus and promote its expulsion, further aiding in the treatment of dry throat ailments. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been shown to provide numerous benefits, including reduced throat irritation, improved respiratory health, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for dry throat conditions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot, then reduce heat to medium.
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool for a few minutes.
Drink the tea while it's warm to soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.)", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, found in products such as Throat Coat, have been shown to provide rapid and temporary relief of sore throat pain in patients with pharyngitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help alleviate dry throat symptoms due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include headaches, dizziness, and an increase in blood pressure in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to consume these teas in moderation, ideally for short periods, and to be aware of the ingredients and potential interactions with other medications, especially those that affect the heart and blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day
Take 1-2 tablespoons of dried root in each cup
Soak the root in boiling water for 5-10 minutes
Strain the tea and discard the root before drinking
Continue for 7-10 days to see improvement in throat health

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat dry throat by soothing and calming the irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of excess mucus. The bioactive constituents of thyme tea, including thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat dry throat include rapid relief from discomfort, reduced coughing, and improved overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried thyme leaves.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves from the 1 tablespoon.
Steep the thyme leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the thyme leaves.
Drink the thyme tea while it's still warm to help soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Wiadomosci lekarskie (Warsaw, Poland : 1960)", Thymus vulgaris teas for dry throat may be beneficial due to its spasmolytic, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, and antioxidant properties, particularly thymol, which can help alleviate cough and other symptoms associated with upper respiratory tract infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can help alleviate dry throat symptoms, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Thymus vulgaris teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming them on an empty stomach, and be cautious when combining them with other medications or herbal supplements to minimize potential interactions and side effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 2-3 times a day
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea
Drink the tea slowly to soothe a dry and irritated throat
Continue for 3-5 days or as directed by a healthcare professional

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial infections, and promote tissue repair. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals can experience relief from dry throat symptoms, such as soreness and discomfort, and improve their overall well-being. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat dry throat ailments include reduced inflammation, enhanced immune function, and a faster recovery time.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make the tea.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of the Echinacea flowers to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea throughout the day to soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Cochrane database of systematic reviews", Echinacea purpurea teas may provide some relief from dry throat due to some preparations being better than placebo in the treatment and prevention of upper respiratory tract infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be an effective treatment for dry throat, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat dry throat, take precautions to avoid overconsumption, as excessive use can lead to increased heart rate, insomnia, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners, diabetes medications, and blood pressure medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup Echinacea purpurea tea, 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons dried root per cup, or as directed.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed.
Drink Echinacea tea for 7-10 days to soothe dry throat.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to the relief of dry throat symptoms. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas can provide relief from dry throat, promote a healthy respiratory system, and support overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Put the Zingiber officinale root in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root.
Add honey to taste, if needed, and drink the tea while it's still warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas for dry throat may be beneficial due to the presence of lariciresinol, a compound found in Z. officinale, which has been shown to inhibit efflux pump activity and enhance the antimicrobial potency of other compounds.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be beneficial in treating dry throat, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its spicy properties and potential allergic reactions. To minimize these side effects, it's essential to consume Zingiber officinale teas in moderation, especially for those with sensitive stomachs or allergies, and to avoid taking it on an empty stomach or before bedtime.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of Zingiber officinale powder with honey 2 times a day.
Use a humidifier to moisten the air and soothe your throat.
Gargle with warm salt water to loosen mucus and ease throat pain.
Rest your voice and avoid shouting or loud talking for 24-48 hours.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry throat by reducing inflammation and irritation in the throat, providing relief from discomfort and pain. The bioactive constituents of rosemary tea, including camphor, bornyl acetate, and ursolic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects, with camphor acting as a natural expectorant to help loosen and clear mucus from the throat. Regular consumption of rosemary tea has been found to provide benefits in treating dry throat, including reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Heat the boiling water in a pot or kettle, then remove from heat.
Add the dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea while it is still warm to help soothe your dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective remedy for dry throat, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it before bedtime or when driving, as it may cause drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/2 cup for first 2 days to check tolerance.
Gradually increase to 1 cup for best results.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey for soothing effect if desired.
Drink tea for 5-7 days for relief from dry throat.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas are a popular herbal remedy used to treat dry throat and soothe irritated mucous membranes. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and combat infections that can exacerbate the condition. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a calming effect on the throat and promote relaxation, which can help to alleviate dry throat symptoms. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas can provide long-term benefits, including improved respiratory health and a reduced risk of complications associated with dry throat.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia.
Steep the dried herbs in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add honey to taste, if needed, to help soothe a dry throat.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, and repeat as needed to help ease a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can help to soothe a dry throat, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, headaches, and digestive issues due to its calming properties and potential interaction with other medications. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to consume Lavandula angustifolia teas in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day, and avoid taking it before driving or operating heavy machinery.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Lavandula angustifolia tea in 1 cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to release its healing properties.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day.
Continue for 2-3 days or until your throat feels better.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their decongestant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry throat by reducing inflammation and congestion, providing relief from irritation and discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, limonene, and alpha-pinene, help to thin mucus and reduce the production of phlegm, making it easier to expel. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas has been shown to provide relief from dry throat symptoms, promote a healthy respiratory system, and support overall well-being.

Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant container.
Add 2 tablespoons of fresh eucalyptus leaves to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink the tea hot or warm to soothe your dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas for dry throat may be beneficial as Eucalyptus globulus was one of the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory disorders in Ethiopia, particularly due to its potential antimicrobial properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can provide relief from dry throat ailments, but they may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to use Eucalyptus globulus teas in moderation, avoid taking them before bedtime, and monitor your body's reaction closely, especially if you have pre-existing conditions or take medications regularly.

Steep in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Strain and discard tea leaves to get the liquid
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day as needed
Repeat daily for up to 7 days to soothe dry throat

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, also known as elderflower tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sambunigrin, help to reduce throat irritation and inflammation, making it easier to swallow and speak. The anti-inflammatory properties of Sambucus nigra teas, particularly the flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to calm the throat and reduce discomfort associated with dry throat ailments. Regular consumption of Sambucus nigra teas has been found to provide relief from dry throat symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Sambucus nigra flowers and leaves.
Combine 1 cup of the flowers and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to release.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea warm or at room temperature to soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), Sambucus nigra teas, which are a component of Throat Coat, significantly reduced the intensity of throat pain when swallowing and provided a rapid, temporary relief of sore throat pain in patients with pharyngitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be effective in treating dry throat ailments, but it may also cause side effects such as allergic reactions, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in high doses or for extended periods. To use Sambucus nigra teas safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid taking it with other medications, and be cautious when consuming it with other herbs that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Sambucus nigra tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 5-10 mL of tincture 2-3 times a day.
Use 20-30 drops of tincture in water 2-3 times a day.
Follow the recommended dosage on the tea package.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been used for centuries to treat dry throat ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry throat by reducing inflammation and providing relief from pain and discomfort, allowing individuals to breathe and swallow more comfortably. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthol and menthone, help to treat dry throat by thinning mucus and reducing congestion, making it easier to clear the airways. The benefits of peppermint tea in treating dry throat include its ability to provide fast and effective relief, promote relaxation, and help to reduce the risk of further irritation and infection.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh mint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 2 tablespoons of fresh mint leaves to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the mint leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the mint leaves.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature as needed to soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", Mentha x piperita teas for dry throat have been traditionally used and recognized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated mucosa.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can help alleviate dry throat symptoms, but possible side effects include digestive issues, such as upset stomach or diarrhea, and allergic reactions like skin irritation or hives in rare cases. To use Mentha x piperita teas safely, individuals should be cautious when consuming it in large quantities, especially for extended periods, as it may cause an imbalance of certain minerals in the body, particularly potassium.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Mentha x piperita tea, 3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard leaves.
Take the tea for 3-5 days to soothe and heal dry throat.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen over time.

The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of Melissa officinalis help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the throat, providing relief from discomfort and pain. preparations.herb_desc: Verbascum thapsus teas, derived from the flowers and leaves of the mullein plant, are a natural remedy for treating dry throat and coughs. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of this herbal preparation help to calm and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the throat. Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while warm, up to 2-3 cups a day for dry throat relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, there is no information about the Verbascum thapsus teas for dry throat. The study focuses on the botanical lozenges made from various medicinal plant extracts, including Siraitia grosvenorii, Lonicera japonica, Platycodon grandiflorus, and Glycyrrhiza uralensis, but Verbascum thapsus is not mentioned.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Verbascum thapsus teas can help alleviate dry throat symptoms, but its use may also cause gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its saponin content. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming Verbascum thapsus teas on an empty stomach to reduce the risk of stomach upset.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat the dry throat ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to the high quinine content. To use this herbal preparation safely, take it in moderation, avoid mixing it with other stimulants, and stay hydrated to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Rheum officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilages present in Rheum officinale help to form a protective layer on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation, and promoting healing of the affected area. Bioactive constituents such as anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids in Rheum officinale teas have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to their therapeutic effects. By consuming Rheum officinale teas, individuals can benefit from a reduction in dry throat symptoms, improved throat health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rheum officinale root. You can buy it at a health food store or online.
Measure 1 cup of water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Add the dried Rheum officinale root to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then turn off the heat.
Strain the tea and drink it while still warm. You can add honey if you prefer it sweeter.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rheum officinale teas can be used to treat dry throat, but it may cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea in large quantities or for extended periods.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion teas, have been used to treat dry throat ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry throat by reducing inflammation and providing relief from discomfort, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable swallowing experience. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to their therapeutic effects by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat dry throat include a reduction in inflammation, improved throat comfort, and a soothing effect on the mucous membranes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Dry the leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Steep the dried leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink while warm to soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Frontiers in pharmacology, Taraxacum officinale teas for dry throat are not mentioned in the provided study, but the study shows that botanical lozenges prepared from extracts of medicinal plants such as Platycodon grandiflorum (which is related to Platycodon grandiflorus mentioned in the study), may help alleviate symptoms of dry throat, with an improvement rate of 67.50% in the experimental group.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat dry throat ailments, but some possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in individuals who are sensitive to plant-based substances. To minimize potential side effects, take precautions such as drinking plenty of water when consuming the tea, starting with small amounts to gauge your tolerance, and avoiding consumption if you have any known allergies or sensitivities to dandelion or other members of the Asteraceae family.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba teas help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, further contributing to the relief of dry throat symptoms. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been found to provide relief from dry throat, alleviate coughing, and promote overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce the heat and let the leaves steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink while warm, as needed to soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat dry throat, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. To safely use Ginkgo biloba teas, take precautions to start with small amounts and avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, as this can exacerbate stomach discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, commonly known as wild marjoram or oregano tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry throat by reducing inflammation and providing a protective layer on the mucous membranes, thereby relieving discomfort and pain. The bioactive constituents of Origanum vulgare, including carvacrol and thymol, exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that aid in combating bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to dry throat. Regular consumption of Origanum vulgare tea can provide relief from dry throat symptoms and promote overall respiratory health, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of fresh Origanum vulgare leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Origanum vulgare leaves to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink the tea while it's warm to soothe a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to BioMed research international, Origanum vulgare teas for dry throat have been shown to exhibit significant antibacterial activity, with a zone inhibition (ZI) of 20-22 mm and a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 3-15.62 μg/mL, suggesting their potential efficacy in soothing a dry throat.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can help alleviate dry throat symptoms, but it may also cause stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interact with certain medications due to its high content of volatile oils. When using Origanum vulgare teas to treat a dry throat, it's essential to drink plenty of water, start with small amounts to assess tolerance, and avoid consuming it before bed as it may cause digestive discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas have been traditionally used to treat dry throat ailments due to their soothing and calming properties. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in the throat, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain. The bioactive constituents of Matricaria chamomilla, including apigenin and luteolin, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Drinking Matricaria chamomilla tea can provide relief from a dry throat by reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and soothing the mucous membranes, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Steep the dried flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to soothe your dry throat.
Drink the tea while warm to help relieve your dry throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Supportive care in cancer : official journal of the Multinational Association of Supportive Care in Cancer, Matricaria chamomilla teas for dry throat may be beneficial due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory actions, which were found to reduce the severity and incidence of lesions, and alleviate pain symptomatology in cancer patients with oral mucositis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be an effective remedy for soothing a dry throat, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with ragweed allergies or taking certain medications. Precautions to take when using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat a dry throat include starting with a low dose, monitoring for any adverse reactions, and avoiding it if you have a history of allergies or are pregnant or breastfeeding.

preparation_study: According to Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for dry throat may be beneficial due to its antimicrobial activity, which has been shown to effectively inhibit bacteria such as S. pyogenes, S. pneumoniae, and S. mutans, commonly causing acute pharyngitis.
intro_study: According to "Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.)", teas for dry throat such as Traditional Medicinals' Throat Coat, a traditional demulcent herbal tea, have been found to provide a rapid, temporary relief of sore throat pain in patients with pharyngitis.
